weather_report:
Just before midnight in Subiaco, the sky is scrubbed clean and quiet, stars pricking the dark. The air has that winter snap that clears the head, and the night feels orderly and transparent, streets washed in a calm, dry stillness.

The forecast keeps the canvas clear through the small hours and into morning, so expect a bright, untroubled start once the sun climbs. UV is low now and will only begin to matter after the day settles in; carry a hat and a touch of sunscreen if you plan to linger outside. It looks pleasantly crisp rather than harsh, the kind of clear day that rewards unhurried errands and a window seat. Dress in easy layers—a warm sweater for the early chill, a light jacket you can shrug off by late morning, sunglasses for the winter glare. Consider a short, quiet stroll before bed, an early coffee at opening time, then a meandering walk to the shops or hanging fresh laundry to drink the light; let the clarity set the rhythm.
